title: Pouteria ceresolii
taxonomy: Pouteria ceresolii is classified within the family Sapotaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the sapote family. This family comprises over 200 species, including various species of trees and shrubs. Pouteria ceresolii is further classified within the genus Pouteria, which includes approximately 25 species of sapotes found primarily in tropical regions of Africa and Asia. The species Pouteria ceresolii is native to Madagascar and is characterized by its unique characteristics and adaptations to its native environment. The classification of Pouteria ceresolii helps scientists understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other species within the Sapotaceae family.
uses: Pouteria ceresolii, also known as the Bolivian Brazil nut tree, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. Its bark and leaves are known to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers, due to its antimicrobial properties.
